Hi there! Get in touch if you need an easy to work with and versatile lead iOS developer for your app or platform that needs good performance, slick user experience, and some kind of AI. I'm a developer and cofounder (indie game) specializing in graphics optimization (Metal/OpenGL), real-time visualizations, and embedding ML models for on-device inference. Experience with LLM fine-tuning, vector search, and converting large models to compact on-device implementations. Led development of an App Store-featured app WikiNodes, and games with multiple awards. Recently built a word game system navigating 1.5M+ words with 110M+ relationships using Metal shaders at 60fps.Seeking remote senior/lead role.Personal site: https://www.pgligierko.com/Recent project: A pathmaking wordplay game: https://www.inotherwords.app

Location: London, UK
  Remote: Yes or hybrid

  Technologies: C#, ASP.NET, Azure, JavaScript, React, TypeScript, SQL, NoSQL, PowerShell, plus many other languages/tools.
  Résumé/CV: Please request by email
  Email: hireme@johnh.co
  
I have 8 years of experience, and I'm comfortable working on both greenfield and brownfield projects. I do well with detail-oriented work. I have 14.7k reputation on StackOverflow and have answered over 300 questions there, mostly on .NET-related topics. (The link to my SO profile is on my CV.)I'm looking for a senior full stack or senior backend role. I was unable to work following issues during the pandemic, which are now resolved, but I'm rarely getting to talk with anyone to be considered for interviews; AI rejects my CV outright. I'm stuck in an algorithmic prison.Despite the length of break, my knowledge is current. If you need convincing of that, I recently got through the whole interview process at Moneybox for a senior backend role, which is pretty much the only interview I've had. Although they hired someone else, they spent a week trying to allocate extra budget to hire me. Unfortunately, that didn't happen, and so here I am.If you aren't hiring, but know someone who is, I'd appreciate you sharing my details with them. I can start straight away. If anyone has any advice to help break out of the AI auto rejections, I'd also appreciate it if you'd contact me via email. Thanks for reading.

I'm an ML researcher @ MIT and a student at Northeastern University majoring in CS and Linguistics. I've built large-scale, efficient software for my neuromechanics and quantum photonics labs, for satellites, and for agentic video editing startups. My most recent projects: I built https://github.com/tetraslam/SHFLA and won the MIT Media Lab hackathon. It hit the front page of HN, and got me demo requests from Stephen Wolfram, David Sinclair, Joscha Bach, and Suno AI. I also built https://mosaic.so's developer API, 4 core tiles (intro, outro, watermark, and AI augment), universal media search (search over hundreds of thousands of videos/images/audio in <2s accurately), and social media agents which autonomously find, edit, and post content to great amounts of engagement (>10k impressions per post in testing)Location: San Francisco, CA / Boston, MARemote: NoWilling to relocate: Within the United States, Singapore, and Japan.Technologies: Python, Zig, C, C++, Langchain/Langgraph, Javascript, Linux, Supabase, Redis, Pytorch, Slurm, CUDA, HPC, FastAPI, Embedded Linux, Tinygrad, Concurrency, Triton, Bash, Typescript, NextJS, TailwindCSS, LangchainResume: https://tetraslam.world/resume.pdfWebsite: https://tetraslam.worldEmail: shresht@mit.edu

Hello there! My name is Julián and I'm passionate about building usable, understandable, scalable and secure abstractions for users and developers. I am a low-level software engineer with extensive (10+ years) experience shipping large, mission-critical, customer-facing software (Apple filesystems and kernel, WebKit) and I'm looking for a new challenge. I have strong history building dependable software in environments from startups to large corporations, and I value opportunities to work with new technologies and languages. Growing teams and mentoring junior engineers are also essential skills on my CV.I am detail-oriented (testing and code comments are crucial), and I value effective and kind communication. I bring a mindset focused on reliability even under ambiguous requirements, and I try to do the "right thing" even when it's difficult. Right now, as side projects I'm working on writing an emulator in Rust, and an app for some task planning.Feel free to send an email or LinkedIn message! I always welcome good conversation and interesting technical challenges.

Location: Detroit, MIRemote: YesWilling to relocate: noTechnologies: Embedded systems, automotive, machine learning, signal processing, data science, real time computing, safety processes (MISRA, ISO26262, etc), MATLAB, FPGA.Resume: LinkedIn -- https://www.linkedin.com/in/wcunningEmail: wdocunningham \@\ gmail.comBackground: Masters in control theory and signal processing, working in the auto industry for the last 7 years, first on low level powertrain software then the last year or so on LIDAR/computer vision for autonomous vehicles. I've done the most work in C, but I'm now contributing to safety development work with the Rust consortium and Ferrous Systems through my current employer. Recently had to read the Linux kernel source and use the BCC tool suite to find a subtle issue in the network stack causing high latency on sensor data for AV, also coincidentally fixed most of the network timing infrastructure.Currently doing software defined vehicle software architecture with a focus on fail-safe and fail-operational, loosely coupled, distributed systems. This includes some standards contributions and work with high level suppliers like Nvidia and Renesas for next-generation software/hardware system co-development.Primary skills include hard real time embedded systems, safety critical applications, firmware development, system integration and complex hardware/software interaction debugging, along with detailed data analysis problems.Side interests include machining, 3D printing, embedded system development, coffee roasting and furniture making.
